---
title: "Bank Lending Standards"
subtitle: "Senior Loan Officer Opinion Survey — Federal Reserve"
description: "The Fed asks senior loan officers if they're tightening credit. Four times a year. We chart it."
toc: true
freeze: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="data-note">
  <strong>Data source:</strong> Federal Reserve Senior Loan Officer Opinion Survey (SLOOS) —
  Table 1 (domestic banks), parsed from Fed HTML tables via the bens-data-lake pipeline.
  Net percentage = % reporting tighter standards minus % reporting easier standards.
  All-respondent segment only.
  See <a href="../data-sources.qmd">Data Sources</a> for methodology.
</div>
```

Four times a year, the Federal Reserve asks senior loan officers at major US banks whether
they've been tightening or loosening lending standards. The result is a diffusion index —
net percentage tightening — which tells you how many banks changed, not by how much.

Sustained readings above +20% have historically preceded things worth paying attention to.
Readings above +40% have historically preceded things you definitely don't want to be
fully invested during.

---

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
import os
ojs_define(r2_base_url=os.environ.get("R2_BASE_URL", ""))
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

import { DuckDBClient } from "npm:@duckdb/duckdb-wasm"
db_sloos = DuckDBClient.of({})
r2Base = r2_base_url
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

// Aggregate net % tightening directly from raw parquet partitions.
// Patterns identify headline standards tables via context_blob (accumulated question text).
sloosRaw = {
  if (!r2Base || r2Base === "") return null
  try {
    const result = await db_sloos.query(`
      WITH classified AS (
        SELECT
          release_code,
          CASE
            WHEN context_blob LIKE '%A. Standards for large and middle-market firms%'
              AND context_blob NOT LIKE '%B. Standards for small firms%'
              AND context_blob NOT LIKE '%a. Maximum size of credit lines%'
              THEN 'C&I - Large'
            WHEN context_blob LIKE '%B. Standards for small firms (annual sales of less than $50 million)%'
              AND context_blob NOT LIKE '%a. Maximum size of credit lines%'
              THEN 'C&I - Small'
            WHEN context_blob LIKE '%credit cards from individuals or households changed%'
              AND context_blob NOT LIKE '%Credit limits%'
              THEN 'Consumer - Credit Card'
            WHEN context_blob LIKE '%and lease financing.)%'
              AND context_blob NOT LIKE '%Maximum maturity%'
              THEN 'Consumer - Auto'
          END AS loan_type,
          SUM(CASE WHEN response_category IN ('Tightened considerably','Tightened somewhat') THEN value ELSE 0 END)
            - SUM(CASE WHEN response_category IN ('Eased somewhat','Eased considerably') THEN value ELSE 0 END)
            AS net_pct_tightening
        FROM read_parquet(
          '${r2Base}/sloos/curated/source=fed_sloos_table_1/release_code=*/observations.parquet',
          union_by_name=true
        )
        WHERE metric = 'percent'
          AND respondent_segment = 'all_respondents'
          AND response_category IN ('Tightened considerably','Tightened somewhat','Eased somewhat','Eased considerably')
        GROUP BY release_code, loan_type
        HAVING loan_type IS NOT NULL
      )
      SELECT
        strptime(release_code::VARCHAR, '%Y%m') AS survey_date,
        loan_type,
        ROUND(net_pct_tightening, 1) AS net_pct_tightening
      FROM classified
      ORDER BY survey_date, loan_type
    `)
    return Array.from(result).map(d => ({
      ...d,
      survey_date: new Date(d.survey_date),
      net_pct_tightening: Number(d.net_pct_tightening)
    }))
  } catch(e) {
    return { error: e.message }
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

LOAN_COLORS = ({
  "C&I - Large":            "#4A6FA5",
  "C&I - Small":            "#7BA3C8",
  "Consumer - Auto":        "#C8A058",
  "Consumer - Credit Card": "#A07860"
})

LOAN_ORDER = ["C&I - Large", "C&I - Small", "Consumer - Auto", "Consumer - Credit Card"]

RECESSIONS = [
  { start: new Date("2001-03-01"), end: new Date("2001-11-01") },
  { start: new Date("2007-12-01"), end: new Date("2009-06-01") },
  { start: new Date("2020-02-01"), end: new Date("2020-04-01") }
]
```

## Latest Reading

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  if (!r2Base || r2Base === "") {
    return html`<div class="chart-loading"><span>◌</span><span>Live data — chart loads when the data lake is ready.</span></div>`
  }
  if (!sloosRaw || sloosRaw.error) {
    return html`<div class="chart-error"><span>⚠</span><span>Data unavailable — ${sloosRaw?.error ?? "check R2 connection"}.</span></div>`
  }

  const byType = {}
  for (const lt of LOAN_ORDER) {
    const rows = sloosRaw.filter(d => d.loan_type === lt).sort((a, b) => b.survey_date - a.survey_date)
    byType[lt] = { latest: rows[0], prior: rows[1] }
  }

  const maxDate = d3.max(sloosRaw, d => d.survey_date)
  const qNum = Math.ceil((maxDate.getMonth() + 1) / 3)
  const qStr = `Q${qNum} ${maxDate.getFullYear()}`

  const cards = LOAN_ORDER.map(lt => {
    const { latest, prior } = byType[lt]
    const val = latest?.net_pct_tightening
    const prev = prior?.net_pct_tightening
    const delta = (val != null && prev != null) ? val - prev : null
    const sign = val >= 0 ? "+" : ""
    const dSign = delta > 0 ? "+" : ""
    const arrow = delta > 0 ? "▲" : delta < 0 ? "▼" : "—"
    const arrowColor = delta > 0 ? "#B04A38" : delta < 0 ? "#3A7A5A" : "#8A8A8A"
    const deltaStr = delta != null
      ? `<div style="font-family:'DM Mono',monospace;font-size:0.63rem;margin-top:0.3rem;color:${arrowColor}">${arrow} ${dSign}${Math.abs(delta).toFixed(1)}pp vs prior qtr</div>`
      : ""
    return `<div class="kpi-card">
      <div class="kpi-value" style="color:${LOAN_COLORS[lt]}">${val != null ? sign + val.toFixed(1) + "%" : "—"}</div>
      <div class="kpi-label">${lt}</div>
      ${deltaStr}
    </div>`
  }).join("")

  return html`<div>
    <div style="font-family:'DM Mono',monospace;font-size:0.68rem;letter-spacing:0.06em;text-transform:uppercase;color:#8A8A8A;margin-bottom:0.5rem">Latest survey: ${qStr}</div>
    <div class="kpi-strip">${cards}</div>
  </div>`
}
```

## Tightening Over Time

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

viewof timeWindow = Inputs.radio(
  ["2Y", "5Y", "All"],
  { value: "All", label: "Period" }
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

viewof loanFilter = Inputs.checkbox(
  LOAN_ORDER,
  { value: LOAN_ORDER, label: "Loan types" }
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

filteredData = {
  if (!sloosRaw || sloosRaw.error) return []
  const cutoff = (() => {
    if (timeWindow === "All") return new Date(0)
    const d = new Date()
    d.setFullYear(d.getFullYear() - parseInt(timeWindow))
    return d
  })()
  return sloosRaw
    .filter(d => loanFilter.includes(d.loan_type))
    .filter(d => d.survey_date >= cutoff)
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  if (!r2Base || r2Base === "") {
    return html`<div class="chart-loading"><span>◌</span><span>Live data — chart loads when the data lake is ready.</span></div>`
  }
  if (!sloosRaw || sloosRaw.error) {
    return html`<div class="chart-error"><span>⚠</span><span>${sloosRaw?.error ?? "unknown error"}</span></div>`
  }
  if (filteredData.length === 0) {
    return html`<div class="chart-loading"><span>—</span><span>No data matches current filters.</span></div>`
  }

  const maxDate = d3.max(filteredData, d => d.survey_date)
  const asOfStr = maxDate
    ? maxDate.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { month: "short", year: "numeric" })
    : "unknown"

  const activeLoanTypes = LOAN_ORDER.filter(lt => filteredData.some(d => d.loan_type === lt))

  return Plot.plot({
    width: 820,
    height: 400,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginRight: 20,
    marginBottom: 50,
    color: {
      domain: activeLoanTypes,
      range: activeLoanTypes.map(lt => LOAN_COLORS[lt]),
      legend: true
    },
    x: {
      type: "time",
      label: null,
      tickFormat: d => d.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { month: "short", year: "2-digit" })
    },
    y: {
      label: "Net % Tightening",
      tickFormat: d => (d > 0 ? "+" : "") + d + "%",
      grid: true,
      gridColor: "var(--border-color, #D8D3C8)"
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.rect(RECESSIONS, {
        x1: "start",
        x2: "end",
        fill: "#8A8A8A",
        fillOpacity: 0.09
      }),
      Plot.ruleY([0], { stroke: "#8A8A8A", strokeWidth: 1, strokeDasharray: "5,3" }),
      Plot.ruleY([20], { stroke: "#C8A058", strokeWidth: 0.8, strokeDasharray: "3,4", strokeOpacity: 0.5 }),
      Plot.line(filteredData, {
        x: "survey_date",
        y: "net_pct_tightening",
        stroke: "loan_type",
        strokeWidth: 2,
        curve: "monotone-x"
      }),
      Plot.dot(filteredData, Plot.selectLast({
        x: "survey_date",
        y: "net_pct_tightening",
        fill: "loan_type",
        r: 4
      })),
      Plot.tip(filteredData, Plot.pointerX({
        x: "survey_date",
        y: "net_pct_tightening",
        stroke: "loan_type"
      }))
    ],
    caption: `Source: Fed SLOOS Table 1 | Net % = tightening minus easing | All respondents | Shaded = NBER recessions | Dashed amber = +20% | As of ${asOfStr}`
  })
}
```

## Last 3 Surveys

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  if (!sloosRaw || sloosRaw.error || !r2Base) return null

  const allDates = [...new Set(sloosRaw.map(d => d.survey_date.toISOString().slice(0, 7)))]
    .sort().slice(-3)

  const recent = sloosRaw.filter(d => allDates.includes(d.survey_date.toISOString().slice(0, 7)))
  const activeLoanTypes = LOAN_ORDER.filter(lt => recent.some(d => d.loan_type === lt))

  return Plot.plot({
    title: "Net % Tightening — Last 3 Surveys",
    width: 820,
    height: 230,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginRight: 80,
    marginBottom: 40,
    color: {
      domain: activeLoanTypes,
      range: activeLoanTypes.map(lt => LOAN_COLORS[lt]),
      legend: true
    },
    x: {
      label: "Net % Tightening",
      tickFormat: d => (d > 0 ? "+" : "") + d + "%",
      grid: true,
      gridColor: "var(--border-color, #D8D3C8)"
    },
    y: { label: null, domain: activeLoanTypes },
    fy: { label: null },
    facet: {
      data: recent,
      y: d => d.survey_date.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { month: "short", year: "numeric" })
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.ruleX([0], { stroke: "#8A8A8A", strokeDasharray: "4,3" }),
      Plot.ruleY(recent, {
        x1: 0,
        x2: "net_pct_tightening",
        y: "loan_type",
        stroke: "loan_type",
        strokeWidth: 3,
        strokeLinecap: "round"
      }),
      Plot.dot(recent, {
        x: "net_pct_tightening",
        y: "loan_type",
        fill: "loan_type",
        r: 6
      }),
      Plot.text(recent, {
        x: "net_pct_tightening",
        y: "loan_type",
        text: d => (d.net_pct_tightening >= 0 ? "+" : "") + d.net_pct_tightening.toFixed(1) + "%",
        dx: d => d.net_pct_tightening >= 0 ? 14 : -14,
        textAnchor: d => d.net_pct_tightening >= 0 ? "start" : "end",
        fontFamily: "DM Mono, monospace",
        fontSize: 11,
        fill: "loan_type"
      })
    ],
    caption: "Source: Fed SLOOS Table 1 | Each panel is one quarterly survey | Net % = tightening minus easing"
  })
}
```

---

## Methodology

**What the number means:** Net tightening = (% of respondents reporting tighter standards) − (% reporting easier standards). A reading of +30 means 30pp more banks tightened than eased — not that 30% of banks tightened.

**What's covered:** Domestic banks, all-respondent segment, headline credit standards questions only. Terms-of-loan tables (spreads, maturities, covenants) are excluded — this is standards, not pricing.

**Historical context:**
- **+40% and above**: Significant tightening — GFC (2008–2009) and COVID (2020).
- **+20% to +40%**: Elevated — late-cycle credit slowdowns.
- **Near zero**: Roughly neutral. **Negative**: Net easing.

**Data coverage:** Fed SLOOS Table 1 HTML tables, quarterly, back to Q3 2018. Source pages at federalreserve.gov/data/sloos.

**Recession shading:** NBER-defined recession dates. Not a forecast.

For full source documentation, see [Data Sources →](../data-sources.qmd)
